title: The Yellowknives
summary: The Yellowknives led by Akaitcho is a custom civilization by TopHatPaladin, with contributions from Lasqueto, Urdnot, Zharques, Civitar, and Patum333.

uniques.body: Strategic Resources, as well as {{Copper Icon}} Copper, provide {{Food Icon}} Food to the city working them. Units receive +1 Sight when beginning their turn in Tundra or Snow.
uniques.name: Long Distances Over Snow
Copper Axeman
bullets: Reveals a Strategic Resource near the tile of any Ancient Ruin it clears

uniques.name: Kwik'ìì Doò
bullets: Gains 5 HP when beginning its turn on a tile with a resource

leader.body: Akaitcho was the chief of the Yellowknives, one of the five First Nations groups of the Dene, who lived north and northeast of the Great Slave Lake. His territory included the region from the eastern portion of the Great Slave Lake, in the Northwest Territories, Canada, to the Coppermine River, in Nunavut. He was recruited to act as interpreter, guide, and hunter for John Franklin's first of three Arctic expeditions, the Coppermine Expedition of 1819–1822.
leader.title: Akaitcho

civilization.body: The Yellowknives, or T'atsaot'ine, are a Dene subgroup, native to the Canadian Shield in the modern-day Northwest Territories. Their traditional lands are north of the Great Slave Lake and along the Coppermine River. The "Yellowknife" name derives from the yellowish copper that the Yellowknives used for most of their tools; this copper is also the source of the Coppermine River's name.
civilization.title: The Yellowknives

dawnOfMan.defeat: "Impossible! We were unbeatable! How could this have happened?"
dawnOfMan.blessing:
"The time to protect your tribe is now, oh indomitable Akaitcho, chief of the Yellowknives. A shrewd leader, you traded supplies with the North West Company's Fort Providence, helping to keep your small band together and provisioned. In 1820, when your tribe numbered about 190, you were recruited by John Franklin and the Royal Navy for the Coppermine Expedition to find the Northwest Passage, in return for debt cancellation, cloth, ammunition, tobacco, and iron products. The journey was harsh, with the trek from Fort Providence to Fort Enterprise sapping morale and left supplies running short. The group reached the Arctic Ocean, and Akaitcho and the Yellowknives left with instructions to leave caches on the way back. But many of your hunters died, you did not receive ammunition from Fort Providence to kill game, and believed most of Franklin's expedition were doomed, and so did not restock the Fort Enterprise. But Franklin refused to blame Akaitcho, as he had not been paid and consistently treated his party with kindness. Throughout the subsequent years, the Yellowknives fortunes reversed, with Fort Providence and trade shutting.
Oh resolute Akaitcho, conditions are still harsh. Cities may now stand in the ruins of the trading posts, but heavy snow and fog still blanket the traditional lands of your people. Will you protect your people and guide them to safety and prosperity? Can you build a civilization that can stand the test of time?"
dawnOfMan.introduction: "There aren't many who have it in them to survive out here! You must be a brave one. The name's Akaitcho, pleased to meet you."
